data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Here are the **legacy patterns** I identified:

| # | Legacy Pattern | Modernization |
|---|---------------|---------------|
| 1 | Non-SDK `<Project>` with `ToolsVersion` & `xmlns` | Use `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Handled by SDK automatically |
| 3 | `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | Unnecessary — SDK defaults |
| 4 | Explicit `Configuration` / `Platform` conditions | SDK defaults are identical |
| 5 | Debug & Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | All values match SDK defaults |
| 6 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | `TargetFramework net472` |
| 7 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK auto-globs all `.cs` files |
| 8 | `<Reference Include="System">` etc. | Implicit for `net472` in SDK-style |
| 9 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Move metadata into `<PropertyGroup>` |
| 10 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Default `true` for libraries in SDK |

Let me apply the modernization now.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>1.0.0.0</FileVersion>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize the project.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Non-SDK-style project format** — uses `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, explicit `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`
2. **Explicit `Compile` items** — SDK-style auto-globs `.cs` files
3. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style auto-generates assembly attributes from project properties
4. **Explicit framework references** (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) — not needed in SDK-style


5. **Configuration-specific property groups and defaults** — SDK-style handles these automatically
6. **`ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic`** — all unnecessary or already defaulted in modern projects
7. **`TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2`** should become `TargetFramework net472`, and properties like `OutputPath`, `DefineConstants`, `ErrorReport`, and `WarningLevel` are all defaults that can be removed

Now I'm going to modernize the project file by converting it to SDK-style format and consolidating the assembly metadata from AssemblyInfo.cs directly into the project file.

data.content:
The project has been modernized from **47 lines → 16 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

**Removed (handled by SDK defaults):**
- `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, XML declaration
- Both `Import` statements (props + targets)
- `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `OutputType` (Library is default)
- `Configuration`/`Platform` condition properties
- Entire Debug & Release `PropertyGroup` blocks (all values were defaults)
- `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`
- All 6 `<Reference>` items (implicit for `net472`)
- All 3 `<Compile>` items (SDK auto-globs `*.cs`)
- `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` → metadata moved into `<PropertyGroup>`

**Kept/converted:**
- `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` → `TargetFramework net472`
- Assembly metadata (description, company, copyright, version) as project properties
